sections.paragraph:
κακοήθης, ες (s. prec. entry; Pla., Menand. et al.; Vett. Val. 47, 2; 4; PGiss 40 II, 11; 4 Macc 1:25; 2:16; Philo, Somn. 2, 192; Jos., Ant. 1, 42; contrast καλοήθης in a list of virtues: Cat. Cod. Astr. XII 100, 9) malicious, spiteful D 2:6.—M-M. s.v. κακοήθεια.

prec. prec. = preceding

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 500
